data.title: Goldman Sachs Says It Will Fund Eligible Employees’ Children’s “Trump Accounts”
data.description:
Goldman Sachs said it will provide funding support for “Trump accounts” for eligible employees’ children.

According to Jin10, the company said the support will be available to employees’ children who meet the eligibility requirements.

data.title: Onyx Protocol to Shut Down Onyx Layer 3 on December 31, 2026, Shifting Focus to Layer 1
data.description:
Onyx Protocol said it will deprecate and shut down Onyx Layer 3, also known as the XCN Ledger, on December 31, 2026, as it shifts development focus entirely to Onyx Layer 1.

According to Foresight News, Onyx said the transition will not change XCN itself.

The protocol said the Ethereum ERC-20 contract will remain the primary representation of XCN, and holders will continue to bridge assets between Ethereum and Onyx Layer 1 as needed.

As part of the transition, Onyx said XCN holders will be able to bridge their Ethereum-based XCN to Onyx Layer 1 to participate in staking, applications, and future network functions.

data.title: U.S. 8-Week Treasury Bill Auction Allocation Falls to 53.99%
data.description:
The U.S. 8-week Treasury bill auction allocation was 53.99% as of July 2.

According to Jin10, the previous reading was 70.5%.

data.title: STOCKS | Yimeng OncoBio Says HKEX Questions Loan Exemption; Independent Approval Needed
data.description:
Yimeng OncoBio Pharmaceutical (1541) said HKEX has questioned whether a partial exemption applies to a loan transaction involving its chairman, according to Ming Pao.

The company, which listed in September 2023, announced in February that it would provide a one-year loan of about RMB13.4247 million to chairman Tian Wenzhi for tax compliance purposes, with interest linked to the National Interbank Funding Center’s one-year quoted rate.

It said HKEX sent a letter on June 24 stating the partial exemption clause does not apply. HKEX said the transaction requires an independent financial adviser’s opinion and approval by independent shareholders.

data.title: Fhenix Acquires FHE Infrastructure Firm Sunscreen, Names Ravital Solomon Research Lead
data.description:
Fhenix, a fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) Layer 2 project, has acquired FHE infrastructure company Sunscreen for an undisclosed amount and appointed Sunscreen founder Ravital Solomon as its head of research. According to Foresight News, Fhenix said the acquisition is expected to strengthen its research and development capabilities in on-chain privacy computing.

Sunscreen focuses on applying FHE technology to blockchain and is described as an early participant in the sector. The company has also developed compiler technology within its ecosystem aimed at lowering the barrier for developers to use encrypted computation.

data.title: U.S. Bitcoin ETFs See Net Outflows While Ethereum ETFs Post Net Inflows, Lookonchain Says
data.description:
U.S. Bitcoin exchange-traded funds recorded net outflows of 6,165 BTC today, while U.S. Ethereum exchange-traded funds posted net inflows of 21,568 ETH, according to Lookonchain monitoring.

According to Odaily, Lookonchain data also showed Bitcoin ETFs had net outflows of 32,807 BTC over the past seven days.

Over the same seven-day period, Ethereum ETFs recorded net outflows of 54,411 ETH, the monitoring data showed.

data.title: AI | China’s Kling AI Raises $2 Billion to Expand AI Video Operations
data.description: China’s Kling AI raised an initial $2 billion in venture capital funding as the Kuaishou Technology spinoff seeks to expand its AI video operations, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Cisco Launches Nexus One Data Center Network Architecture for AI Workloads
data.description:
Cisco has announced the launch of Cisco Nexus One, an open data center network architecture designed for AI workloads and new security threats, aimed at upgrading its long-evolving Application Centric Infrastructure (ACI) system.

According to Odaily, Cisco said Nexus One will connect to its unified platform, Cisco Cloud Control, to improve architectural flexibility, openness, and scalability while maintaining backward compatibility with existing systems.

Cisco added that Nexus One is not a single product but an architecture-level evolution similar to ACI. The company said the focus is shifting from early-stage innovation to standardization and interoperability across ecosystems to meet complex networking needs in the AI and machine learning era.

data.title: BOE’s Mann Says She’s Ready to Hike if Price Pressures Persist
data.description: Bank of England rate-setter Catherine Mann said she is prepared to make an “activist” interest-rate hike if inflation expectations and other warning signs on prices do not improve later this year, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: STOCKS | Fullwealth Holdings to Change Hands After Offer at HK$0.6545 a Share
data.description:
Slope engineering contractor Fullwealth Holdings (2295) said its controlling stake will change hands after Greensun Global Energy, led by Zhu Liming, agreed to buy 300 million shares, or 75%, from chairman Tse Shing Kei and CEO Ho Ka Kei at HK$0.6545 per share, according to Ming Pao.

The total consideration is more than HK$196 million. The offer price represents a 65.6% discount to the closing price of HK$1.9 before trading was suspended on June 22.

Zhu will then make a mandatory general offer to Fullwealth Holdings at HK$0.6546 per share. The company’s shares are set to resume trading tomorrow morning.

data.title: Denmark’s Central Bank Bought 700 Million Danish Kroner in FX Intervention, Market Sources Say
data.description: Market sources said Denmark’s central bank carried out foreign-exchange intervention last month. According to Jin10, the central bank bought 700 million Danish kroner through the intervention.

data.title: AI Debt Boom Makes US Credit Look Safer While Masking Risk
data.description:
The US corporate bond market is appearing safer as more highly rated technology companies issue debt tied to the AI boom, according to Bloomberg.

Some investors are warning that the improved credit profile could be misleading and may be masking underlying risks.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Kuaishou Says Investors Agreed to Inject $2.03 Billion Into Beijing Keling
data.description:
Kuaishou said it signed a capital increase agreement involving Beijing Keling and multiple investors, under which investors agreed to make cash capital injections totaling RMB 13,823.60 million (or $2,028.20 million), subject to conditions being met or waived.

According to Jin10, Kuaishou said the agreement was signed after trading hours on July 2, 2026, by (i) Beijing Keling, an indirectly wholly owned subsidiary of the company that is expected to hold Kuaishou Group’s Keling AI-related assets and business after the restructuring is completed; (ii) 21 independent investors, Party A and Party B; (iii) Beijing Keling Group Company; (iv) LuckyLabs; and (v) Beijing Kuailingrui.

Kuaishou said that, with Beijing Keling’s consent, additional investors may become parties to the agreement by signing a joinder agreement within 60 days from the signing date. It said the total amount of capital increase under the agreement must not exceed RMB 20,447.10 million.

Kuaishou said that on the same day as the capital increase agreement, 15 additional investors each signed joinder agreements with Beijing Keling, becoming parties to the agreement and agreeing to inject a combined RMB 5,223.50 million in cash capital, subject to conditions being met or waived.

Kuaishou said Beijing Keling may, within the subscription period and within the subscription cap, enter into further joinder agreements with new additional investors.

data.title: WTI Slides Below $68 as Saudi Exports Surge; BTC Tops $61,500, Gold Above $4,000
data.description:
Oil’s selloff deepened Thursday, with WTI crude slipping below $68 for the first time in 125 days as Saudi shipments through the reopened Strait of Hormuz erased much of the war premium, according to BeInCrypto, after prices had topped $110 at the height of the conflict. Four Bahri supertankers reportedly left the Gulf carrying about 8 million barrels, while Saudi Aramco resumed loadings at Ras Tanura after a near four-month halt.

Bitcoin (BTC) rose more than 5% to above $61,500, while gold extended gains beyond $4,000, with the metal near $4,119.

data.title: WORLD CUP | Starmer rejects Tuchel plea for kids to miss school for England-Mexico 1am kick-off
data.description: UK Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer rejected England manager Thomas Tuchel’s call for parents to “write an excuse” so children can watch England’s early-morning World Cup match against Mexico, with his spokesperson saying children “should be in school on Monday,” according to Yahoo Sports. England’s win over the Democratic Republic of Congo on Wednesday set up the Mexico City clash with co-hosts Mexico, which kicks off at 1am BST; some schools have said they will allow later drop-offs or screen replays, while others plan to open as normal.

data.title: $32.9 Billion in Tokenized Assets Showed No Weekly Transfers, Report Finds
data.description:
A report found that $32.9 billion in tokenized assets recorded no transfer activity over a one-week period, highlighting uneven usage across the sector.

According to NS3.AI, the findings were based on a BeInCrypto report that examined activity in the real-world asset (RWA) tokenization market.

The report said the inactive assets accounted for about 56% of the roughly $60 billion RWA tokenization market.

It added that low transfer activity does not necessarily indicate a product has failed, noting that some tokenized products are not designed for secondary market trading.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | US-Iran Deal Reopens Strait of Hormuz After February War
data.description:
The US and Iran struck a deal on June 17 to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, the world’s most important route for shipping oil and natural gas, according to Bloomberg.

The strait had been largely blocked after the two countries went to war in February.

data.title: STOCKS | Nasdaq 100 Futures Fall 1% as Nasdaq Turns Lower
data.description: Nasdaq 100 index futures fell as much as 1%. According to Jin10, the Nasdaq erased its earlier gains and was down 0.2%, while the S&P 500’s rise narrowed to 0.2% and the Dow was up 0.7%.

data.title: STOCKS | Apple Shares Rise 4%
data.description: Apple shares rose 4%. According to Jin10, the stock was up 4%.

data.title: U.S. Natural Gas Futures Slip 1.6% After Inventory Build Exceeds Expectations
data.description:
U.S. natural gas futures edged lower, falling 1.6%, after a U.S. Energy Information Administration report showed that the increase in inventories exceeded expectations.

According to Jin10, the report indicated that stockpiles rose by more than the market had anticipated.
